relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Religious authorities come from Jerusalem to challenge Jesus.
They focus on handwashing tradition.
Jesus turns the question toward human tradition overriding divine command.
Honor of parents is named as divine instruction.
A religious loophole can hide neglect.
Tradition can empty the word when used without mercy.
Isaiah’s word exposes hypocrisy.
Lips can be near while the heart is far.
Human commands can masquerade as worship.
Jesus calls the crowd to hear and understand.
Defilement is not a simple outside-in issue; the mouth reveals the inner life.
The disciples notice the offense his word caused.
Only what the Father plants will remain.
Blind guidance leads into a pit.
Peter asks for the parable’s meaning.
Jesus presses them toward understanding.
Food passes through the body.
Speech arises from the heart and reveals what is there.
Harmful patterns emerge from the heart before they become action.
The true purity question is heart-output, not ritual hand status.
Jesus withdraws toward Tyre and Sidon.
A Canaanite woman cries for mercy for her daughter.
Silence and disciple annoyance test the encounter.
The stated mission boundary is Israel’s lost sheep.
She persists in worship and asks for help.
The boundary is spoken in hard table language.
She answers from within the image and finds room for mercy under the table.
Jesus names her trust great, and her daughter is healed.
Jesus returns to Galilee and sits on the mountain.
Crowds bring every kind of need to his feet.
Healing becomes visible and Israel’s Divine is glorified.
Compassion notices hunger after three days.
The disciples see lack in a deserted place.
Jesus asks what they have.
The crowd reclines on the ground.
Thanks, breaking, and giving turn little into enough.
All eat, all are filled, and seven baskets remain.
The feeding includes more than the counted men.
Jesus releases the crowd and crosses by boat.
